design_philosophy.core_loop: Select character → Battle through arcade ladder of opponents → Execute special moves and combos → Defeat final boss Shao Kahn → Perform finishing move (Fatality/Animality/Friendship)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mastering input-based combat mechanics (combos, Run meter management, spacing) against escalating AI opponent difficulty with character-specific movesets
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a martial arts warrior fighting to stop Shao Kahn's invasion of Earthrealm; express dominance through brutal finishing moves and stylish combat sequences
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: MK3 is deliberately visceral and spectacle-driven: it prioritizes gore/Fatalities as core fantasy elements, introduces pre-programmed 'dial-a-combos' to lower execution barrier while maintaining depth, and implements Run button to balance defensive play (addressing perceived franchise imbalance). The 'Choose Your Destiny' difficulty selector respects player skill variance. Kombat Kodes system transforms competitive play into a metagame of unlocking hidden mechanics, treating code-hunting as legitimate engagement rather than cheat exploitation.

difficulty.continues_system: Arcade-style continue system; Ultimate Kombat Kode (10-character code entered on game over screen) unlocks playable Smoke without continuing
difficulty.checkpointing: Arcade ladder structure: progressive opponent battles with no mid-ladder checkpoints; defeat forces continue or restart
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency via 'Choose Your Destiny' screen in single-player mode allowing difficulty selection; Kombat Kodes (6-symbol pre-match codes) modify gameplay difficulty, AI behavior, and mechanics (e.g., health adjustments, timer manipulation via unused Slow/Fast option in other ports)

progression.upgrade_system: No persistent character upgrades; progression is through learning matchup knowledge and execution mastery across arcade ladder
progression.unlock_mechanism: Kombat Kodes unlock hidden characters (Smoke via Ultimate Kombat Kode), cosmetic features (All Background Blast, All Projectiles Bounce Back), and gameplay modifiers (Shadow Fighting Enabled, Nintendo Power Exclusive practice mode). Codes revealed through gaming magazines and promotional material
replayability_hooks: 22-character roster with distinct movelists and playstyles encourages learning different fighters
Numerous Kombat Kodes create variable match conditions and hidden content
Interactive stages (ceiling breaks via uppercuts, level transitions mid-battle) add environmental unpredictability
Finishing move variety (Fatalities, Animalities, Friendships, Stage Fatalities) incentivizes repeated playthroughs with different characters
Mercy → Animality sequence adds high-skill execution goal
Two-player versus mode with Kombat Kode modification system

world_building_techniques: Arcade ladder narrative: each character has implied motivation to fight Shao Kahn (invasion of Earthrealm narrative frame)
Character select screen and versus intros establish tone and faction (Earthrealm warriors vs. Shao Kahn's forces)
Stage environments (Subway, Bell Tower, Pit 3, etc.) communicate setting and atmosphere
Finishing moves (Fatalities, Animalities) serve as narrative punctuation expressing character identity
Kombat Kodes include thematic messages (e.g., 'THE VORTEX' hidden stage where Smoke is fought)
Story avoids tournament framework of predecessors, replacing it with invasion/resistance narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Shao Kahn positioned as final antagonist with higher stats and moveset threat than regular roster; represents the external invasion threat. AI difficulty scaling creates escalating opponent challenge across ladder
notable_bosses: Shao Kahn (final boss, arcade ladder conclusion)
Smoke (hidden character, Ultimate Kombat Kode unlock, robotic variant)

faction_indicators: Earthrealm warriors (Liu Kang, Sonya Blade, Jax, Kung Lao, etc.) vs. Shao Kahn's invasion force
Character selection communicates player alignment with resistance or otherworldly threat
Finishing moves (especially Fatalities) express faction brutality or honor depending on character choice
danger_signals: Shao Kahn invasion metanarrative establishes existential threat
Sindel resurrection and collaboration with Shao Kahn implies betrayal/corruption themes
Character omissions (Scorpion notably absent) suggest hidden conflicts or character fates
Interactive stages featuring environmental hazards (ceiling breaks, stage transitions) communicate unstable/hostile world state
environmental_storytelling: Stage design (Subway, Bell Tower, Pit variants) implies global invasion impact across multiple locations
Interactive ceiling breaks and level transitions suggest dimensional instability during conflict
Stage Fatalities embedded in environments communicate environmental threat integration
The Vortex hidden stage (unused internal name) implies secret/hidden dimensions or pocket realms in lore

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor) - Mortal Kombat 3 (SNES) article covering port stability, hidden content, Kombat Kodes, unused features
wikipedia - Mortal Kombat 3 article covering gameplay mechanics, narrative context, character roster, release information, development notes
